phrases.context: France and Italy are both in the Schengen Area, so passport checks are rare but can occur. Border agents may stop vehicles — simply present your document.

phrases.french: Je suis touriste — je visite l'Italie pour la journée.
phrases.english: I am a tourist — I am visiting Italy for the day.
phrases.phonetic: zhuh swee too-REEST — zhuh vee-ZEET lee-TAH-lee poor lah zhoor-NAY
phrases.context: Day trips to Ventimiglia market (Fridays) are very popular from Menton. The border is 4km east of Menton old town.

phrases.french: Où est le marché de Vintimille ?
phrases.phonetic: oo eh luh mar-SHAY duh van-tee-MEE
phrases.context: The famous Friday market in Ventimiglia is one of the largest outdoor markets on the Riviera — great for Italian produce, clothing, and leather goods.

vocabulary.english: Ventimiglia (French name for the Italian border town)

tips: Ventimiglia's Friday market is unmissable — huge outdoor market with Italian food, leather, clothing, and produce
The border crossing is open 24/7; spot checks do occur so carry ID or passport
Walking across the border is easy — the old town of Ventimiglia is about 1km from the French side
